changed_functions.name: AfdServiceWaitForListen @ 0x1C00197C0 (patched 0x140043C80)
key_changes: Bounds-check block refactored from goto-based sequence into one compound conditional, but the conditions are logically identical in both builds (when flag bit 0x100 is clear, only the primary size *(a2+0xa0) is checked; when set, primary+6 and, if the secondary maximum is non-zero, secondary+6 are checked)
Copy source is *(a2+0x98)+6 with length *(*(a2+0x98)+4)+2 in BOTH builds — no change to the buffer copy source or length
Reference-count increment hardened: unpatched 'lock inc dword ptr [r14+38h]' (32-bit) became patched 'lock xadd [r14+40h], rax' (64-bit) followed by '__fastfail(0xE)' when the resulting count is <= 1
Struct field offsets shifted (endpoint context grew), accounting for most of the byte-level diff
changed_functions.security_notes: The only behavioral change in this function is the reference-count integrity fastfail. The bounds-check logic is equivalent between builds and the MDL-mapped copy uses the same validated pointer *(a2+0x98) with the same length in both builds. There is no out-of-bounds read/write in this path. An earlier hypothesis of a skipped secondary-buffer check leading to an OOB write is not supported: the 'secondary not checked when bit 0x100 clear' branch exists identically in the patched build.

changed_functions.name: AfdRestartBufferReceiveWithUserIrp @ 0x1C0005A10 (patched 0x14000C0B0)
key_changes: Reference-count increment at [obj+0x30] changed from 'lock inc' (32-bit, result ignored) to 'lock xadd' (64-bit InterlockedIncrement64) followed by '__fastfail(0xE)' when the new count is <= 1
Both builds already use atomic (lock-prefixed) reference-count operations — there is no non-atomic decrement
Struct field offsets shifted due to context-structure growth
changed_functions.security_notes: This AFD receive-repost path takes a reference on the connection object (a3+0x30) before adding the repost work item via AfdLRListAddItem. The unpatched build increments the 32-bit count without checking the result. The patched build performs a 64-bit interlocked increment and executes __fastfail(0xE) (FAST_FAIL_INVALID_REFERENCE_COUNT) if the resulting count is not greater than 1, i.e. if the reference was taken on an object whose count had already reached 0. This is defense-in-depth against reference-count-based use-after-free. The report's earlier 'non-atomic dec then if(old==1) free' double-free mechanism is not present in this function.

changed_functions.name: AfdTLTPacketsSend @ 0x1C003EFE8 (patched 0x140053CE8)
key_changes: Two reference-count increments (fields +0x30 and +0x38 -> +0x40) changed from 'lock inc' (32-bit) to checked 64-bit increments with '__fastfail(0xE)' when the new count is <= 1
Allocation modernized from ExAllocatePoolWithQuotaTag(0x30, 'AfdF') to ExAllocatePool2(0x61, 48, 'AfdF') — identical size (0x30 = 48) and identical tag (0x46646641 = 'AfdF')
Struct field offsets shifted due to context-structure growth
changed_functions.security_notes: This TDI transport send/registration path takes references on connection objects. The unpatched build uses plain 32-bit interlocked increments; the patched build uses checked 64-bit increments that __fastfail(0xE) on a resulting count <= 1, consistent with the driver-wide reference-count integrity hardening. The allocation change is a straight ExAllocatePoolWithQuotaTag -> ExAllocatePool2 API modernization: size (0x30) and pool tag ('AfdF') are unchanged. The earlier claim of a corrected undersized allocation (0x210) is not supported by the binaries.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0048484 (AfdRioCreateRqPair)
key_changes: Registered-I/O buffer registration: IoAllocateMdl + MmProbeAndLockPages + MmMapLockedPagesSpecifyCache for user-supplied buffers
Uses AfdRioCheckRqBufferSize (0x1C0047030) to validate count*0x48+0x10 == total_size with count <= 0x38E38E2 (integer-overflow guard)
Error paths consolidated between builds
changed_functions.security_notes: Registers I/O buffers for AFD registered I/O. The size validation guards against integer overflow in count*struct_size. No new security-relevant defect identified in the diff; the change is a consolidation of error handling.

changed_functions.name: WskKnrCompletePending @ 0x1C0013068
key_changes: Reference-count decrement handling restructured; fields consolidated between builds
Consistent with the driver-wide reference-count integrity hardening pass
ETW logging parameters changed
changed_functions.security_notes: Resource cleanup with reference counting. Changes are consistent with the broad reference-count hardening; no independent exploitable change identified.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C003D7B4 (AfdFastTransmitFile)
key_changes: Fast-path file send using FsRtlCopyRead / FsRtlMdlRead
Sums three sizes and uses an int-sized allocation length on the buffered path; the large-transfer path uses MDL-based I/O
Additional validation lines between builds
changed_functions.security_notes: Fast TransmitFile path. No security-relevant change confirmed in the diff. The earlier reference to a maximum-size constant at 0x1C00582D0 is not verifiable against the binaries and has been removed.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0047030 (AfdRioCheckRqBufferSize)
key_changes: Size validation helper: checks arg1 * 0x48 + 0x10 == arg2 with arg1 <= 0x38E38E2
Prevents integer overflow in count * struct_size arithmetic
changed_functions.security_notes: Integer-overflow guard; equivalent logic in both builds.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C000AC10 (AFDETW_TRACERECVDATAGRAM)
key_changes: ETW trace logging function
Register renaming and data-reference changes only

changed_functions.name: sub_1C00751B0 (WskTdiCreateAO)
key_changes: Device file creation via IoCreateFileEx with ECP list
Allocation wrapper changed between builds

changed_functions.name: sub_1C00668D0 (AfdCommonDelAddressHandler)
key_changes: List management function
Register renaming; equivalent logic for list unlinking and cleanup

security_findings.function: Reference-count increments across AFD (AfdServiceWaitForListen @ 0x1C00197C0, AfdRestartBufferReceiveWithUserIrp @ 0x1C0005A10, AfdTLTPacketsSend @ 0x1C003EFE8, and ~225 further sites)
security_findings.entry_point: Any AFD path that takes a new reference on an endpoint/connection/receive-repost object
call_chain: Object teardown drives a reference count toward 0

A concurrent path (pending I/O completion, receive repost via AfdRestartBufferReceiveWithUserIrp, TDI send via AfdTLTPacketsSend, accept/listen servicing via AfdServiceWaitForListen) takes a fresh reference on the same object
security_findings.description: The dominant security-relevant change is a driver-wide rollout of reference-count integrity checks. In the unpatched build, taking a new reference is a plain 32-bit interlocked increment (lock inc dword ptr [obj+off]) whose result is ignored. In the patched build the increment is performed as a 64-bit interlocked add (lock xadd) and the result is checked: if the new count is not greater than 1, the code executes __fastfail(0xE) (int 29h with ecx=0xE, FAST_FAIL_INVALID_REFERENCE_COUNT). This halts the machine when a reference is taken on an object whose count had already reached 0, a hallmark of use-after-free / reference-count underflow. The unpatched build has this check at 2 sites; the patched build has it at 227. This is defense-in-depth hardening: the diff shows the guard being added but does not by itself demonstrate a reachable, attacker-controllable underflow.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Reaching the fastfail requires an AFD object's reference count to already be at or below 0 when a new reference is taken. A candidate race: (1) create a TCP socket via WSASocket; (2) issue an overlapped WSARecv so a receive-repost context is live; (3) from a second thread call closesocket() while the receive is pending, racing endpoint teardown against the reference-taking paths. On a patched machine, if the race drives the count to 0 before a reference is re-taken, __fastfail(0xE) fires (bugcheck 0x139, subcode 0xE). Deterministic reproduction is not established from the diff.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrated. The check is a mitigation: it converts a would-be use-after-free into an immediate, non-exploitable bugcheck rather than exposing an out-of-bounds or controllable-free primitive.

breakpoints.why: Break at the 'lock xadd [rbx+30h], rax' increment. Read [rbx+0x30] beforehand; a value of 0 or negative means the following cmp/jg will route to the fastfail.
The 'int 29h' with ecx=0Eh (FAST_FAIL_INVALID_REFERENCE_COUNT). Reached only when a reference is taken on an object whose count was already <= 0.
The unpatched 'lock inc dword ptr [rdi+30h]' with no following check; the reference is taken even if [rdi+0x30] was already 0.

watch: [obj+0x30]: reference count field being incremented (rdi in unpatched, rbx in patched AfdRestartBufferReceiveWithUserIrp)
Bugcheck code on a patched machine: 0x139 (KERNEL_SECURITY_CHECK_FAILURE) with subcode 0xE
Whether any concurrent teardown path can drive the count to 0 before this increment (the underflow precondition)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Run a tight loop of overlapped WSARecv immediately followed by closesocket() from separate threads across many sockets to maximize the teardown-vs-reference race window. Note that reaching the fastfail requires an actual reference-count underflow, which is not demonstrated by the diff.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: On patched: an immediate __fastfail-driven bugcheck (0x139, subcode 0xE) if and only if the race drives a reference count to 0 before a new reference is taken. On unpatched: no fastfail; the count is silently incremented from 0, leaving a revived (dangling) object that may be used after free later.
debugging_aids.notes: The __fastfail(0xE) / FAST_FAIL_INVALID_REFERENCE_COUNT pattern is a runtime integrity check applied broadly (227 sites in the patched build vs 2 in the unpatched build). It stops exploitation of a reference-count underflow at the point of detection; it does not remove a root-cause logic defect, because none is visible in this diff.

call_chain: NtDeviceIoControlFile -> AfdDispatch -> AfdWaitForListen handler -> AfdServiceWaitForListen (MDL-mapped copy of accept data)
security_findings.description: An earlier hypothesis of an out-of-bounds read/write in this function via a skipped secondary-buffer bounds check is not supported by the binaries. The bounds checks are logically identical between builds: when endpoint flag bit 0x100 is clear, both builds check only the primary size field (*(a2+0xa0)) and do not check the secondary field; when bit 0x100 is set, both builds additionally check *(a2+0xa0)+6 and (if the secondary maximum is non-zero) *(a2+0xa8)+6. The patched build expresses these as one compound conditional rather than a goto sequence, but the truth table is the same. The MDL-mapped copy reads its source from the same validated pointer *(a2+0x98) with length *(*(a2+0x98)+4)+2 in both builds. The only behavioral change in this function is the reference-count integrity fastfail covered by the primary finding.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not applicable; no buffer-validation defect exists in this path.

breakpoints.why: Entry point. Inspect the bounds checks and the memmove source (*(a2+0x98)+6) / length (*(*(a2+0x98)+4)+2); confirm they match between builds.

watch: FsContext+8: endpoint flags; bit 0x100 selects which (equivalent) validation branch runs
*(a2+0x98): validated buffer pointer used as the copy source in BOTH builds
*(a2+0xa0): primary size field; *(a2+0xa8): secondary size field
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Not applicable; retained only to document that the previously suspected OOB path is not exploitable.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: The copy parameters (destination VA, source *(a2+0x98)+6, length *(*(a2+0x98)+4)+2) are identical between unpatched and patched at the same code point.
debugging_aids.notes: This finding is downgraded to informational: the buffer validation is unchanged and no out-of-bounds primitive exists. The function's only real change is the reference-count integrity fastfail (see the primary finding).

summary: The diff between afd_unpatched.sys and afd_patched.sys (Windows Ancillary Function Driver) is dominated by a driver-wide reference-count integrity hardening pass. Plain 32-bit interlocked reference-count increments (lock inc) were replaced with 64-bit interlocked increments (lock xadd) followed by __fastfail(0xE) (FAST_FAIL_INVALID_REFERENCE_COUNT, int 29h with ecx=0xE) when the resulting count is not greater than 1. This halts the machine when a reference is taken on an object whose count had already reached 0, a defense-in-depth mitigation against reference-count-based use-after-free. The check appears at 227 sites in the patched build versus 2 in the unpatched build, including in AfdServiceWaitForListen, AfdRestartBufferReceiveWithUserIrp, and AfdTLTPacketsSend. The previously reported out-of-bounds write in AfdServiceWaitForListen is not supported by the binaries: the bounds checks are logically identical between builds and the copy source is the same validated pointer *(a2+0x98) in both. The previously reported 'non-atomic decrement / double-free' mechanism is also not present: both builds use atomic operations, and the real change is a checked increment (not a decrement guard) with fastfail code 0xE (not 0xD). The AfdTLTPacketsSend allocation change is an ExAllocatePoolWithQuotaTag -> ExAllocatePool2 API modernization with identical size (0x30) and tag ('AfdF'), not an undersized-allocation fix. The reference-count hardening is genuine but defense-in-depth; the diff does not demonstrate a reachable, attacker-controllable underflow.
